_comment: Example data file for scripts/render_receipt.py. This is a fictional, fully generic illustration — none of the names, topics, or numbers below refer to any real person, company, or event. Fill in real values from your own session analysis, then run: python3 scripts/render_receipt.py this_file.json output_basename
period_label: last 30 sessions (spans 2026-06-21 - 2026-07-20, ~30 days, active on at least 9 of them)
total_note: Counts distinct files, docs, decks, skills, and artifacts produced, not sessions. A chat draft and a finished deck both count as one "shipped" item each, even though they aren't comparable units of work.

topics.name: Skill and tool building
topics.deliverables: 1 new skill, 1 updated skill, 1 live artifact

topics.name: IT and access administration
topics.deliverables: 1 config edit, 1 audit answer, 1 escalation draft

what_shipped: 10 deliverables produced: a slide deck, a board-ready competitive brief, a new installable skill, a live triage artifact, a customized skill, a reference guide, an updated roster, a doc fix, a revised feedback draft, and a diagram.
10 digests posted to chat automatically, one per weekday run, with no one asking each time.
Several message drafts prepared for review across a vendor security review, an IT escalation, and an internal announcement.
framing: A third of this window is autonomous: the recurring digest runs unattended each weekday and produces a decision-ready summary without anyone asking for it that day. The rest splits between external-facing deliverables, incident response, and investment in the tool itself. For a manager audience, lead with the tangible deliverables rather than the session count — sessions include routine automation, deliverables don't.
table_notes: Deliverables are counted per topic and don't roll up into one "total files" figure the way a codebase diffstat would.
Percentages are of session count; Cowork doesn't expose per-session time or spend to weight this by effort instead.
what_counts: This reads session transcripts and lists what was produced. It does not compute cost or "hours saved" — a dollar figure would be inferred from token counts rather than measured, and a saved-hours multiplier has no real baseline to compare against. Pull spend figures from the Console if you need them.
privacy_note: Transcripts can contain real conversation content, not just file diffs, so this describes activity rather than quoting conversations back.
footer_note: Claude Chat (claude.ai) is not included — no tool available here reads that conversation history, only local Cowork sessions on this machine.
